module.name: executor
module.purpose: Executor folder module for host-side tool execution. executor.rs defines Executor, whose new(agent_tx) stores the shared agent SessionMessage sender alongside a ToolRegistry (built via ToolRegistry::new()) and an empty WorkQueue (WorkQueue::new()) of active ExecutionRuntime records keyed by ExeId, and now exposes a single public router entry point route_session_event alongside preview: route_session_event destructures SessionEvent::Execution(signature, execution_event) (ignoring non-execution events) and either intercepts ExecutionEvent::Evoke(request) into the private create_execution or passes every other execution event to the private forward_to_execution. preview delegates to the registry. The private create_execution resolves the requested tool via registry.get by request.signature.name, clones the Tool, builds ExecutionRuntime::new(tool, request, agent_tx.clone()) tracked in the WorkQueue by request.signature.exe_id, and on an unknown tool reports ExecutionStatus::Failed back to the agent through the private emit_failed helper (which locks agent_tx, a std::sync::Mutex, packages the event into a SessionMessage via HostSession::package_message, and try_sends a SessionEvent::Execution Status event); the private forward_to_execution obtains the target ExecutionRuntime's local control Sender via self.executions.with(&signature.exe_id, |runtime| runtime.sender()) and, when the execution is tracked, sends the reconstructed SessionEvent::Execution(signature, execution_event) into that runtime's event loop (which handles Cancel/Kill and other control events; the local per-execution control channel stays SessionEvent). The Executor exposes no per-operation methods and holds no local updates channel; per-execution status and ExecutionUpdate values stream to the agent directly from each ExecutionRuntime via agent_tx (packaged into SessionMessage via HostSession::package_message), so the Executor only routes events and tracks live runtimes. registry.rs is now implemented: ToolRegistry is a collection of available host Tool handles, whose new() loads the global Config and registers every tool that loads successfully from the configured tool directory (config.tool.directory), skipping duplicate-name collisions during the scan; register rejects a tool whose name is already present with RegistryError::DuplicateName, get performs a linear lookup by name, and preview maps each registered tool to its ToolPreview. RegistryError reports registration failures such as DuplicateName. The execution child module implements the per-execution runtime: ExecutionRuntime (the host-side runtime of an agent Execution holding an Arc<ExecutionContext>, its execution_tx SessionEvent sender, and a worker JoinHandle spawned in new with no separate run) runs the Tool via ExecutionContext and streams ExecutionStatus and ExecutionUpdate to the agent, while ExecutionContext holds the host-local Tool handle (crate::executor::Tool, defined in tool.rs), the ExecutionRequest (whose signature identifies the execution), and the shared agent SessionMessage sender that is locked via std::sync::Mutex, packaged via HostSession::package_message, and driven with try_send. tool.rs defines Tool, the Executor-side handle to an external tool executable located by filesystem path: Tool derives Clone, load spawns the executable with --preview and caches the parsed ToolPreview (returning None on any spawn/exit/parse failure, so load returns Option<Self>), name/description/input_schema/output_schema/preview read the cached preview, and execute spawns the executable with --run <input> and returns the trimmed stdout; the ToolRegistry holds these Tool handles and hands an owned Tool to each execution. The compiled tool executables are built by the separate top-level marix-tool crate, which host no longer depends on at compile time. mod.rs re-exports the executor items (now including Tool) and is otherwise wiring only.

childModules.name: execution
childModules.purpose: Per-execution host runtime split out of executor.rs: ExecutionRuntime drives one agent Execution, spawning a worker in new (no separate run) that reads the raw tool input from parameters.tool_request (Option<String>), runs tool.execute, and emits ExecutionUpdate plus terminal ExecutionStatus while handling Cancel/Kill control events; ExecutionContext holds the host-local Tool handle (crate::executor::Tool, defined in host/executor/tool.rs), the ExecutionRequest (whose signature identifies the execution), and agent SessionMessage sender, which is locked via std::sync::Mutex, packaged via HostSession::package_message, and driven with try_send; the local per-execution control channel stays SessionEvent.
